Okay. So it is 11:15 on Wednesday. I’m just about to start actually writing
copy. I have until about 1:00 right now, so 1 hour and 45 minutes which is less

useful, so pay attention.
(00:27)
First thing, we left off yesterday is we finished the brief, which was who’s the
audience, 40 to 65-year-old women who are significantly overweight. Family
is important, they’ve lost weight multiple times in the past, they doubt if they
can ever permanently lose weight, they’ve been burned so many times, they
generally don’t like being prescribed meds, don’t trust their doctors, they try
to act tough, but they’re sensitive about their weight. Short-term is just being
fat, overweight, feeling like there’s no way to change things, feeling like it’s
hopeless, feeling despair, feeling full of self-doubt and low self-esteem. Then
longer-term, fears of blood sugar, type 2 diabetes, being told they have 10
years or less to live, leaving their families behind, all this other stuff, too.
(01:11)
Reversing the pain point. I’m going to show you why being overweight is not
your fault. How you’re addicted to foods that keep you fat intentionally and
by that I mean, people are intentionally keeping them addicted. Plus, how to
break free of this addiction without any withdrawal or discomfort and put
your body into fat-burning mode 24/7 using the keto diet. Not just any keto

Paradoxical questions. “If low diets help with obesity, heart disease, diabetes
and bad cholesterol, then why have all these things skyrocketed over the
last 50 years since we’ve been eating that way? Why do we get cravings for
sweets, chips and junk foods, but we’ll rarely get cravings for string beans,

and vegetables aren’t.
(04:46)
Metaphors, we have two. “Imagine if a pain pill addict could replace those pills
with a super vitamin, super pill. Not feeling withdrawal, but instead feeling
more energized, clear-headed, focused and have a stabler mood.” More stable;
I can’t say stabler. Put “more stable mood.” “It’s like using an oil tanker to fill
up a gas tank in a Honda Accord. The Honda can take maybe 14 gallons of
gas, but a tanker truck holds 9,000 gallons. So if the tanker keeps pumping
gas into your car after the fuel tank is full, it’ll spill back out, or if it’s high
pressure, it might flood the car and do damage to the engine and all the other
important parts of the car. The car won’t drive very well, if it drives at all, and
before long, the car will break down.
(05:53)
Same with carbs and sugar. Your body can handle a small amount of these
two things and stores them in muscle tissues and vital organs, so they can be
used as energy. But when you’re eating lots of carbs and lots of sugar, your
body becomes flooded. At this time, you produce a bunch of insulin and all the
extra carbs/sugar get stored as fat in your problem areas as well as your liver
or tissues, et cetera - this makes you overweight, causes diabetes, and other
long-term health complications.”
(05:46)
